descriptive_fit_status.headline: Benchmark-local search found no admissible descriptive equation for this target, so the overview should not pretend the operator line is a fit. Dominant rejection code: description_gain_non_positive.

forecast.headline: Forecast change projection is only available when the active target is raw price levels.

change_atlas.headline: Forecast change projection is only available when the active target is raw price levels.

target.analysis_note: Daily returns are the recommended default for analytical inspection. They make drift and persistence weaker, so descriptive fits have to earn their structure.
target.description: Predict the close-to-close fractional return for each day.
target.label: Daily Return
target.recommended_reason: Better default for interpretable descriptive equations because it makes level persistence explicit instead of hiding it inside raw prices.

holistic_equation.honesty_note: Holistic equation combines the compact deterministic lane with an exact residual-closure term over the observed sample. It is a descriptive closure, not a publishable predictive-within-scope claim.

cautions: These are local filesystem paths on the machine that created the run; they may not exist elsewhere.
The presence of a file does not mean its contents were published as accepted results.
No separate probabilistic artifact paths were provided in the supplied context, so this page should not imply that such outputs exist.
Use the page statuses, not just the file names, to judge what was actually accepted.
artifacts.headline: The saved files show where the data, run record, point manifest, and benchmark report live on disk.
key_takeaways: workspace_root is the parent folder for the whole run.
analysis.json is the top-level structured record of the analysis.
spy-daily-return.csv is the dataset file used for the target series.
fmp-history.json is the raw history artifact.
operator-point.yaml maps to the operator point lane.
fmp_spy_benchmark.md maps to the benchmark page.
artifacts.narrative: The artifacts page links the workbench pages back to actual saved files. The workspace root for this run is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return, which is the folder containing the run outputs. The file /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return/analysis.json is the main machine-readable analysis record for the run. The dataset behind the pages is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return/datasets/spy-daily-return.csv. The raw source history is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return/fmp-history.json. The point-lane manifest is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return/manifests/operator-point.yaml, which is the natural file to inspect for the operator point setup and outputs. The benchmark comparison report is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return/benchmark-root/reports/predictive_generalization/fmp_spy_benchmark.md. Taken together, these files map cleanly onto the pages: data files support the overview, the point manifest supports the point page, and the markdown benchmark report supports the benchmark page.
artifacts.summary: This run's workspace root is /Users/danielbloom/Desktop/euclid/build/workbench/20260418T154949Z-spy-daily-return. Within it, analysis.json is the main structured analysis record, spy-daily-return.csv is the dataset used by the pages, fmp-history.json is the raw history file, operator-point.yaml is the point-lane manifest, and fmp_spy_benchmark.md is the benchmark report.

terms.meaning: The main folder containing all files saved for one workbench run.
A structured file that records configuration or outputs for a lane.
The underlying historical data artifact before higher-level page summaries are built.
The saved comparison report used for the benchmark page.

cautions: Do not confuse the operator's tested linear trend with an accepted benchmark descriptive fit; the benchmark page says no admissible descriptive equation was found.
No benchmark-local winner means there is nothing here to rank above the operator result.
The benchmark finding is about descriptive admissibility, while the operator abstention is about publication gates; both are negative, but for different reasons.
Because descriptive_fit is null, the overview should not present any equation as an accepted summary of the target.
benchmark.headline: The benchmark search did not find any admissible descriptive winner, and it does not rescue the operator result.
key_takeaways: Benchmark-local search found no admissible descriptive equation.
The main benchmark rejection code was description_gain_non_positive.
Four benchmark submitters abstained.
There is no winner candidate and no winner submitter on the benchmark page.
The benchmark page does not overturn the operator lane's abstained publication status.
benchmark.narrative: The benchmark page is about local comparison, not about overriding publication status. Here, the benchmark-local search explicitly found no admissible descriptive equation. Its headline says the overview should not pretend the operator line is a fit, and the dominant rejection code is description_gain_non_positive. In plain English, the benchmark process did not see enough descriptive improvement to accept any equation for this target. The status is absent_no_admissible_candidate, 4 submitters abstained, and both winner_candidate_id and winner_submitter_id are null. That matters because it reinforces the same broad message seen elsewhere: this daily-return target did not support an accepted simple descriptive structure in this run. It also does not change the operator outcome. The operator publication block repeats that replay verified a candidate, but publication status was still abstained for robustness_failed and perturbation_protocol_failed. So there is no benchmark-local winner and no operator publication. The two pages agree in outcome, even though they are answering different questions.
benchmark.summary: Benchmark-local search found no admissible descriptive equation for this target. The dominant rejection code was description_gain_non_positive, 4 submitters abstained, and no winner candidate or submitter was selected. The operator lane remains abstained for its own publication reasons.

terms.meaning: A comparison process that checks candidate descriptions within the benchmark framework for this specific run.
Accepted as meeting the benchmark's minimum requirements.
The candidate did not provide positive descriptive improvement, so it was rejected.
A benchmark participant did not produce an acceptable candidate for selection.

cautions: Do not treat the operator candidate as a published result; the lane abstained.
Do not infer a reliable descriptive pattern from the overview, because no admissible descriptive equation was accepted.
The percent_change field is not very intuitive here because the series starts negative and ends positive; the absolute change of 0.01941 is the clearer fact.
There is no probabilistic output to use for uncertainty, intervals, or event chances in this run.
overview.headline: This run looked at 1,255 daily SPY returns, but it ended without a publishable model.
key_takeaways: The sample covers 1,255 daily returns from 2021-04-20 to 2026-04-17.
The series mean was 0.000486 per day, while the standard deviation was 0.010796, so noise was much larger than average drift.
Observed daily returns ranged from -0.058543 to 0.105019.
No descriptive fit was accepted on the overview page.
The operator point lane abstained after replay because robustness and perturbation checks failed.
No probabilistic lane completed or passed calibration.
overview.narrative: The overview says to focus on what the target itself did and on the main reading caution. Here, the target is daily close-to-close fractional return for SPY. Across 1,255 rows, the latest value was 0.01209 versus a first value of -0.00732, an absolute change of 0.01941. The average daily return was small at 0.000486 compared with a much larger day-to-day spread of 0.01080, and the sample included both a -0.05854 low and a 0.10502 high. That is consistent with the stated semantic warning: daily returns weaken drift and persistence, so any claimed structure has to earn its way. In this run, it did not. There was no accepted descriptive equation on the overview page. The operator lane headline says replay verified a candidate, but the point lane still did not clear publication gates because robustness_failed and perturbation_protocol_failed were triggered. The probabilistic summary is also empty: 0 completed lanes, 0 calibration passes, and 0 thin-evidence lanes. So the holistic reading is simple: there was some candidate structure explored, but nothing from this run survived the standards needed to present it as a dependable result.
overview.summary: From 2021-04-20 to 2026-04-17, the target moved from -0.00732 to 0.01209, with a mean daily return of 0.000486 and standard deviation of 0.01080. The series had large day-to-day swings, ranging from -0.05854 to 0.10502. No descriptive fit was accepted, the operator point lane abstained after failing robustness checks, and no probabilistic lane completed.

terms.meaning: The fractional change from one close to the next trading day's close.
A simple equation meant to summarize the shape or pattern in the observed target series.
The system chose not to publish a result because its checks were not passed.
Whether a candidate still looks acceptable under additional validation checks, not just in one replay.

cautions: Do not use the linear trend as an endorsed signal; it failed publication gates.
The slope is very small compared with the observed day-to-day volatility, so the fitted line should not be mistaken for a strong effect.
Because the search scope was limited to the current-release surface, this page does not claim a broad search over many model families.
The failure reasons matter: robustness_failed and perturbation_protocol_failed mean the candidate did not hold up under required checks.
point.headline: A linear trend candidate was found and replayed, but it was not publishable.
key_takeaways: The point lane chose the linear_trend family.
The displayed candidate equation is y(t) = 0.000072 + 0.000001*t.
The search only covered four simple families: constant, drift, linear_trend, and seasonal_naive.
Replay verification happened, but publication still failed.
The formal publication status for the point lane is abstained.
point.narrative: The point page is about the candidate the operator lane found and whether it cleared publication. The selected family was linear_trend, and the fitted equation shown is y(t) = 0.000072 + 0.000001*t. In plain English, that says the candidate expected a very small positive baseline return and then added an even smaller upward slope over time. Given 1,255 rows, the slope term is tiny: if t advances by one step per row, the fitted level only rises by about 0.001255 from start to end. That is a modest pattern relative to the observed standard deviation of 0.010796 and the much wider realized range from -0.058543 to 0.105019. The page also makes clear that the search scope was narrow: only the current-release surface was searched, namely constant, drift, linear_trend, and seasonal_naive. Most importantly, the run did not publish this line. The exact publication headline says operator replay verified a candidate, but the point lane did not clear publication gates. The stated reasons were robustness_failed and perturbation_protocol_failed. So this equation is best read as the candidate that was checked, not as an approved forecasting rule.
point.summary: The point lane selected the linear_trend family from a limited search over constant, drift, linear_trend, and seasonal_naive. Its equation was y(t) = 0.000072 + 0.000001*t. Even so, publication status was abstained because robustness_failed and perturbation_protocol_failed were raised.

terms.meaning: The part of the workbench that tries to produce one best-value prediction or simple point estimate model.
A straight-line pattern over time, with a fixed starting level and fixed slope.
The time index used in the equation, advancing step by step through the rows.
A check that tests whether a candidate remains acceptable under controlled changes or stress conditions.

cautions: Do not infer uncertainty estimates from this run; none were produced.
A failed lane is not the same as a calibrated model with poor scores; here the outputs are missing rather than weakly acceptable.
There is no probabilistic result to combine with the point-lane candidate.
Because calibration fields are null, this page cannot support claims about reliability of predicted probabilities or intervals.
probabilistic.headline: There were no usable probabilistic results in this run.
key_takeaways: All four probabilistic lanes have status failed.
No probabilistic lane completed.
No probabilistic lane passed calibration.
No lane is marked as thin evidence; the result is simply absence, not a weak pass.
There are no equations, selected families, or evidence headlines for these lanes.
probabilistic.narrative: The probabilistic page is meant to explain uncertainty-focused outputs and how well they calibrated. Here, there is almost nothing to interpret because every listed probabilistic lane failed. Distribution failed. Event probability failed. Interval failed. Quantile failed. For each lane, calibration status is null, equation is null, evidence headline is null, search scope headline is null, and selected family is null. The page summary confirms that none of the probabilistic work reached a usable stage: completed_lane_count is 0, passed_calibration_lane_count is 0, and thin_evidence_lane_count is 0. That means this run offers no published distribution of outcomes, no event odds, no prediction intervals, and no quantile forecasts. It also means there is no calibration evidence to lean on, positive or negative, because the lanes did not produce completed candidates to evaluate that way.
probabilistic.summary: All four probabilistic lanes—distribution, event_probability, interval, and quantile—show status failed. The summary counts are 0 completed lanes, 0 passed calibration lanes, and 0 thin-evidence lanes.

terms.meaning: A modeling path that predicts uncertainty, such as full distributions, intervals, quantiles, or event chances.
How well predicted probabilities or intervals match what actually happens.
A predicted range that is supposed to contain the future value with some stated frequency.
A cutoff value such as a median or upper-tail level from a predictive distribution.

publication.headline: Operator replay verified a candidate, but the point lane did not clear publication gates.

search_scope.headline: Operator point lane only searched the current-release surface (constant, drift, linear_trend, seasonal_naive).
